text.title: Marketing Learnings Database Schema Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Marketing Learnings Database Schema MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Schema MUST contain test_id, hypothesis, variant, result, p_value, why_explanation, where_applicable, owner, ts. Missing any field rejects the entry.
rule.rationale: Each field answers a question a future searcher will ask; dropping any field defeats compounding.

rule.statement: hypothesis field MUST be falsifiable (one verb, one variable, one expected direction). Vague hypotheses are rejected.
rule.rationale: Falsifiable hypotheses are the only kind that yield reusable learnings; 'try a new headline' yields nothing.

rule.statement: result field MUST cite the dashboard URL or query that produced the metric. Result without source is fabrication risk.
rule.rationale: Future readers must reproduce the result; un-sourced numbers are not learnings, they are folklore.

rule.statement: where_applicable field MUST list scope conditions (channel, segment, season, funnel-stage). Empty scope = entry rejected.
rule.rationale: Marketing learnings rarely generalize; without scope the entry is harmful when applied out of context.

rule.statement: Named curator MUST review and tag new entries weekly; entries not tagged within 14 days auto-archive.
rule.rationale: Un-curated DBs rot to noise within a quarter; the review ritual is the schema.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
